Spending Habits · What the data says
🚨
July was a collapse
$244 in income against $2,500+ in expenses. Balance fell to $414. Nothing came in. Loom rent, PECO, subscriptions, food — all hit regardless. This is what no pipeline safety net looks like.
⚠️
February and May were also danger months
Feb: $500 in. May: $600 in. Both months your fixed costs (~$1,800-2,000) swallowed everything. Income was sporadic all year — no reliable floor until Yay Clay payroll stabilized in Q3.
📊
Income was feast/famine all year
Strong months: Mar ($4.2K), Apr ($4.2K), Jun ($5K), Aug ($4.5K). Weak months: Feb ($500), May ($600), Jul ($244). Average gross was $2,705/mo but the volatility is brutal — 6 months below $3K, 6 months above.
💸
Apple Cash SENT is a quiet drain
You sent ~$3,500-4,000 via Apple Cash to other people throughout 2025 — multiple $200-500 transfers scattered across every month. This doesn't show up as a clean line item but it's one of your biggest annual outflows. Worth tracking intentionally.
2025 Tax Estimate
rough numbers — not tax advice, talk to a CPA
Income Breakdown for Tax Purposes
Yay Clay W-2
~$7,400 · taxes withheld by them
Bootstrap Media W-2
~$2,000 · taxes withheld by them
Total W-2
~$9,400
Freelance / SE income
~$23,066
Studio rental income
Likely included in SE above
Cash deposits
$2,844 — clarify source, may be taxable
Rough Tax Estimate on SE Income
SE income
~$23,066
SE tax (15.3% × 92.35%)
~$3,257
½ SE deduction
−$1,628
Adjusted gross (approx)
~$30,838
Federal income tax (est. 12%)
~$2,100
NJ state tax (est. 5.5%)
~$1,400
Total tax estimate~$6,757 on SE
Less W-2 withholdings already paid. Business deductions (ManyChat, Adobe, Canva, equipment, Pixieset, etc.) could reduce SE income significantly. Talk to a CPA — this is an estimate only.
✅
Business deductions you should track
ManyChat $600+, Adobe $150, Canva $180, Turboscribe $240, DFY Reels $348, ElevenLabs $132, Heroku $72, Opus Clip $190, Pixieset $160, home office if applicable, equipment, travel for shoots. These reduce your taxable SE income dollar for dollar.
📋
Get a CPA if you don't have one
With this income mix — W-2 payroll + SE freelance + studio rental + cash deposits — you have a complicated return. A good CPA will find deductions that pay for themselves many times over. This analysis is a starting point only.

💡
30% tax rule
Every dollar in — 30% goes to taxes automatically. "Spendable" is what you actually have. Don't touch the tax reserve.
🏢
APF PP Loom LLC = Nova Lounge rent · ~$710/mo
Fixed overhead. $8,500+/year. Studio needs to generate enough to cover this plus your cut. At $75/hr the lounge needs to run ~10hrs/month just to break even on rent alone.
⚠️
February balance hit $382.63
Only $1,262 came in vs $2,895 out. Yay Clay payroll was the only reliable source that month. Twin Sister Docs signing ($1,250/mo) would stabilize this significantly.
✅
Prism Water Pipes is an active client
$946.47 for the 420 campaign shoot (Feb) + renting the lounge. Should be in your Clients tab as a real relationship — photo/video work + studio rental.

Yap App

🔒 Internal use only. This is your pricing brain. The client never sees this — they see a clean proposal after you run the numbers.
Your market position:
Emerging = building your book · Established = proven track record · Premium = top tier, selective
Step 1 — What are you pricing?
Video
Photo
Recurring / Systems
Studio + Strategy

Shoot duration
People involved

Step 3 — Deliverables + Editing
Deliverable type
📸 Photo details
Output quality
Quantity
🎬 Video details
Video type
Quantity
Color grading
Creative direction (undercharge risk ⚠)
Usage Rights — how will they use this content?
biggest single price lever · never skip this question
Organic social is free. Paid ads and commercial use are licensed separately. This multiplies your entire rate.
💡 Example: A $1,500 shoot becomes $1,500 organic · $1,950 paid ads · $2,400 full commercial. Same day. Same work. Ask every time.

📋 Crew Rate Reference
Production
PA — $150–$300/day
Director / AD — $500–$1K/day
Location Mgr — $200–$400/day
Camera / Capture
BTS Photo — $300–$600/day
BTS Video — $400–$800/day
2nd Shooter — $300–$600/day
Drone Op — $400–$800/day
Talent / Style
Lighting Tech — $200–$400/day
Hair / MUA — $200–$500/day
Stylist — $300–$600/day
Markup rule: +15–20% above what you pay. On $2K crew at 20% markup = bill $2,400, pocket $400 just for managing the team.
📥 First reply — inbound inquiry
Hey [name], thanks for reaching out. I'd love to learn more about what you're working on. Can you tell me a bit about the project — timeline, what you're trying to capture, and whether you have a budget in mind? That'll help me put something together that actually makes sense for you.
📋 After scoping — sending quote
Hey [name], great talking. Based on what you shared I've put together a proposal: [scope]. Investment: $[amount]. That includes [deliverables]. To get started I require a 50% deposit ($[half]) which locks in your date and gets us moving. Let me know if you want to adjust anything or if you're ready to proceed.
🤝 Negotiating — holding your rate
I hear you on the budget. The rate reflects the time, gear, and editing that goes into making this look the way it does. What I can do is [adjust scope / reduce deliverables] to bring it closer to [lower number]. If that doesn't work we can revisit when the budget opens up — no hard feelings either way.
✅ Deposit paid — onboarding
Deposit received — you're locked in. Here's what happens next: [shoot date / next step]. I'll send a brief before we meet so we're aligned on the vision. If anything changes on your end just give me 48 hours notice. Looking forward to it.
📦 Delivery + final invoice
Here's your final [gallery / video link]: [link]. I've attached the final invoice for the remaining balance ($[amount]) — payment due within 7 days. Please review and let me know if anything needs adjusting. It was great working with you on this.
💤 Ghosted — follow-up
Hey [name], just circling back on the [project]. No worries if the timing isn't right — just want to make sure this didn't fall through the cracks. If you want to move forward I'm still available [date range]. Otherwise happy to reconnect when you're ready.
💰 Money Split Settings
Chase cut (%)
Default 50% — adjust after Mike convo
NovaLight LLC cut (%)
Goes into the LLC, not your pocket
Monthly overhead ($)
Loom rent + ops — allocated across bookings

TD Bank · Dec 14, 2022 — Dec 13, 2023 · 12 Statement Periods
2023 Full Financial Analysis
Income classification · deductibility · red flags · tax scenarios
Total deposits
$44,073
all inflows incl. self-transfers
Total spent
$38,057
all outflows
Net balance change
+$863
$2,380 → $3,243
Confirmed biz income
~$4,362
Grayson Sky, NovaLight, FB, Gumroad
Monthly Cash Flow
⚠ Nov 2023: Balance hit $21.55 — one missed payment would have bounced
Key Findings
Month by Month
Income classification: Of $44,073 total deposits, ~$7,474 is self-transfers (own money cycling), ~$10,143 is undocumented checks (taxable until proven otherwise). Real identifiable business income: ~$18,000–$21,000.
Income by Source & Tax Status
Expense Categories — Deductibility Flags
Subscription Stack
Running $150–$250/mo in subscriptions whether or not revenue came in.
Total identifiable 2023 deductions: ~$7,883 · Est. tax savings at 30%: ~$2,365
Business Deductions — Confidence Level
Deductions you are probably missing
→ Chase CC statements may contain additional business purchases — pull all card statements
→ Mileage log for NJ → Philly studio runs (65.5¢/mile in 2023)
→ Home office if you use a dedicated space for content creation
→ PECO electricity — if you can document studio sq footage, deduct proportional share
→ Phone bill (not shown) — if used for business, 50–100% deductible
→ Cash ATM withdrawals ($3,807) — if spent on business supplies, need receipts
→ Vivint security — fully deductible if at studio address
→ Business meals with clients at any restaurant where client was present
Disclaimer: Financial analysis, not tax advice. Work with a CPA who understands creative business structures. Figures are based purely on transaction data.
Action Items — In Order of Priority
📊 True Business P&L
💸 Fixed Cost Floor
What you spend every month whether or not you earn a dollar
🎯 Income Concentration
How dependent are you on any one source?
📅 Seasonal Pattern

2022 Report — Waiting for Data
No 2022 bank statements have been uploaded yet. When you have your TD Bank statements for Jan–Dec 2022, paste the transaction data into a new Claude chat and ask for the same analysis format. Then bring the numbers here.
To generate this report:
1. Download your 2022 TD Bank statements (all 12 months)
2. Upload them to Claude in a new chat
3. Ask: "Give me the same financial analysis format as my 2023 report — income classification, deductibility flags, red flags, tax scenarios"
4. Bring back the numbers to update this tab
📊
2021 Report — Waiting for Data
No 2021 bank statements on file. Same process as 2022 — download statements, upload to Claude, get the analysis, update here.
This was the year most creative businesses were rebuilding post-COVID. Understanding what you actually made and spent will give you baseline context for where the growth started.
📊
2020 Report — Waiting for Data
No 2020 bank statements on file. 2020 is worth recovering — it establishes the pre-scale baseline for everything that followed.
TD Bank typically keeps 7 years of statement history accessible online. Login → Statements → select year → download each month as PDF or export transactions as CSV.

2023 — Building Year
Gross in: $44,073
Total out: $38,057
Balance: $2,380 → $3,243 (+$863)
Confirmed biz: ~$4,362
Biggest client: Grayson Sky $3,890
Lowest balance: $21.55 (Nov)
Key risk: $10,143 undocumented deposits
Tax flag: Studio + personal completely commingled
2024 — Decline Year
Gross in: $35,771
Total out: $38,057
Balance: $3,243 → $958 (-$2,285)
Identifiable biz: ~$18,000–$20,000
Biggest client: Grayson Sky $7,800+
Lowest balance: $317 (Jan 30)
Key event: August PECO $968 + double Loom
Tax flag: $5,000 ATM deposit undocumented
2025 — Stabilizing Year
Gross in: $51,330
Total out: $49,392
Balance: $958 → $2,896 (+$1,938)
Identifiable biz: ~$25,000
Anchor: Yay Clay $1,000/mo (from Jul)
Lowest balance: $414 (Jul 13)
Jun/Jul: near-total income collapse
Tax flag: $12,844 unclassified cash
Nova Lounge Rent — APF PP Loom LLC
2023
$3,711
Aug–Dec only (studio started mid-year)
2024
$8,276
Full 11 payments (double payment in Aug)
2025
$8,277
Full year $650–810/mo
Total paid to APF PP Loom LLC across 2023–2025: ~$20,264 — all from a personal checking account, all paid in post-tax dollars, all potentially deductible as a business expense if properly documented. This is the single most important expense to get categorized with your CPA.
2020 · 2021 · 2022 — Unlock These Reports
TD Bank keeps 7 years of statement history. All three years are accessible online right now.
Login → Statements & Documents → select year → download all months → upload to Claude → paste the analysis here.
